assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: Section 2 describes: 'Search the returned comments for any whose body starts with the marker string defined in `shared/description-digest-protocol.md`: [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:'. The marker string and protocol reference are both present.

assertion_results.text: The response describes detecting a mismatch between the stored digest and the computed digest of the current description
assertion_results.evidence: Section 7 'Compare Hex Digests — MISMATCH DETECTED' states: 'The hex digests do not match' and shows 'Expected (from comment): sha256-md:0000...0000' vs 'Actual (computed): sha256-md:<computed-hash>'. The mismatch is clearly detected and reported.

assertion_results.text: The response alerts the user that the description was modified after plan-feature created the task and displays the expected vs actual digest values
assertion_results.evidence: Section 8 'Alert the User' displays: 'The description of TC-9201 has been modified since plan-feature created this task.' It shows 'Expected digest (recorded by plan-feature): sha256-md:0000...0000' and 'Actual digest (computed from current description): sha256-md:<computed-hash>'.

assertion_results.text: The response offers the user a choice: (1) proceed with the current description, or (2) stop so they can re-run plan-feature
assertion_results.evidence: Section 8 presents two options: '1. Proceed — Continue implementing with the current (modified) description as-is' and '2. Stop — Abort implementation so you can review the changes and re-run plan-feature to regenerate tasks from the updated feature description'.

assertion_results.text: The response stops execution and does not proceed to Step 2 or implementation planning until the user responds — following the same pause-and-ask pattern as the incomplete description handling
assertion_results.evidence: Section 9 'STOP Execution — Await User Response' explicitly states: 'Execution halts immediately at this point. No subsequent steps are performed until the user responds' and lists Steps 2-5 as NOT executed. It concludes: 'The skill waits for the user's explicit choice before taking any further action. This follows the same pause-and-ask pattern used for incomplete description handling.'
